segments.s064-g25: This is a simple nominal series naming seasons/months as subjects: بهار (spring), تیرماه (the month Tir), and خزان (autumn) are listed and linked by و (and). The verb آرد (classical 3rd-person present of آوردن) follows and can govern the series; in classical Persian a verb in the singular commonly follows a coordinated subject list.
segments.s064-g26: برآرد is another present tense third-person verb (برآوردن / برآرد) meaning 'bring forth' or 'produce'; پر از is the adjective phrase 'full of'. Here از introduces the complement (what it is full of), میوه‌دار is an adjectival compound 'fruit-bearing/fruitful', and رزان is modifying or qualifying that phrase. The clause describes these times as producing things filled with fruit.
segments.s064-g27: جوان is the subject 'youth' and داردش is the verb-with-enclitic: دارد ('has') + ـش (object clitic 'it' or 'him/it'), so ‘youth has it’. گاه is an adverb 'occasionally/sometimes' placed after the verb here, and با رنگ و بوی is a prepositional phrase 'with color and scent' describing how youth 'has it' (i.e. appears).
segments.s064-g28: گهش functions as a temporal adverb 'at times/then' (گه ± clitic); پیر is 'old/old age' and بینی here is the secondary verb-form meaning 'you see' (classical present 2nd/3rd-person sense 'one sees' or the poetic parenthetical 'you see'). کرده is the perfect participle of کردن used with روی 'face' to form 'has made (his) face [be] sullen' (دژم = 'sullen, grim'). The participle + noun order is a common poetic pattern meaning 'having made the face sullen'.
segments.s064-g29: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from' or 'concerning', linking فرمان (command) and رایش (his counsel/opinion) as the domain whose authority is asserted. کسی نگذرد is a negated present: نگذرد (negative of گذرَدن) means 'does not pass/transgress' and with کسی (anyone) yields the sense 'no one disobeys/oversteps' — a negative universal statement common in older Persian phrasing.
segments.s064-g30: This clause is idiomatic and elliptical. پی مور uses پی in the sense 'foot/track' (the step of an ant) and مور 'ant', followed by بی او 'without him/without his', and زمین نسپرد with نسپرد (سپردن, here negated) meaning 'does not entrust/hand over' or idiomatically 'does not move/take a step'. Read together: even an ant will not set foot on the earth without him — i.e., nothing happens without his leave. Word order is classical (object/phrase before verb) and نسپرد is in the present-negative form.
segments.s064-g31: بدانگه is a temporal connective 'thereupon/then'; که introduces the subordinate clause 'that/when'. لوح آفرید is a verb phrase in which آفرید is the past tense 'created' and لوح ('tablet', here the heavenly/inscribed tablet) is the object; و قلم 'and the pen' continues the list of created instruments.
segments.s064-g32: بزد is the past/simple perfect verb 'he struck/struck with' (here idiomatically 'he wrote/inscribed'); بر is the preposition 'on/upon', همه بودنیها is the object 'all beings/that which exists' (همه = all; بودنیها = those that are), and رقم is the noun 'inscription/mark' (handwriting). The clause says he inscribed/wrote the destiny/mark upon all that exists.
segments.s064-g33: جهان را is a fronted, definite object/topic (را marks the direct object/emphatic theme) and literally 'the world'. فزایش is a noun 'increase/augmentation' that serves as the direct object of the verb. ز جفت is a prepositional phrase 'from/of pair(s)' (ز = 'from'), and آفرید is the past tense verb 'created' placed at the end in typical classical Persian word order, with the agent (God) omitted.
segments.s064-g34: که introduces a subordinate clause 'that/for', followed by the source phrase از یک فزونی 'from a single increase/excess'. نیاید is a negative present/subjunctive-form verb ('does not / should not come'), with the prefixed ن- negating آمد-derived stem; پدید is a verbal/adverbial element 'into being / appear' placed after the verb in poetic inversion.
segments.s064-g35: ز چرخ بلند is an adverbial phrase 'from the lofty heaven' (ز = 'from', چرخ بلند = 'high/lofty heaven'). اندر آمد is the verb phrase 'came in/arrived' with آمد as the main verb; سخن (speech/word) is the thing that came and appears after the verb due to poetic inversion (normal prose order would place the subject before the verb).
segments.s064-g36: سراسر is an adverb 'throughout/entirely' modifying the clause. همین است is a cleft-like copular phrase 'this is exactly / this is the very thing' (است = 'is'). گیتی ز بن places the noun 'the world' before its source phrase 'from the root' (ز بن), a compact predication meaning 'the world is from the root' with the copula understood from همین است.
segments.s064-g37: زمانه is the subject 'the age/era' and به مردم is a dative/benefactive phrase 'for the people' or 'in the eyes of the people'. شد آراسته is a periphrastic past 'became adorned' (شد = 'became', آراسته = 'adorned'), with the adjective following the auxiliary, a normal classical construction.
segments.s064-g38: وزو here functions as an archaic/poetic connector meaning roughly 'and from it' (classical contraction of و ز او), linking to the previous clause. ارج گیرد is a present/habitual phrase 'honor takes / honor is taken' (گیرد = present-tense or modal 'takes/is taken'), همی is an adverb of continuity 'always/still', and خواسته is a past-participle/gerund meaning 'sought/desired' giving the sense 'honor has always been sought from it.'
segments.s064-g39: اگر introduces a conditional. نیستی here is used as an abstract/state noun 'nonexistence / absence' ('if there were no …'), so the clause reads 'if there is no pair in the world' rather than a literal second-person 'you are not'. جفت اندر جهان is the noun phrase 'a pair in the world' (اندر = 'in').
segments.s064-g40: بماندی is an optative/conditional form 'would remain / might remain' used here to express the consequence of the if-clause. توانای is a predicate adjective 'capable / powerful', and اندر نهان is a locative phrase 'in secret / hiddenly'; the order places the modal/result verb before the predicate in classical poetic style.
segments.s064-g1: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when' introducing the clause. خورشید تابان is noun + postpositive adjective ('the shining sun'); برآمد is a past-tense, 3rd-person singular verb 'rose/appeared' used in literary Persian; ز کوه is a prepositional phrase 'from the mountain' (ز is the poetic form of از). Word order is subject + adjective, then verb, then source phrase.
segments.s064-g2: برفتند is past-tense, 3rd-person plural 'they departed/they went' and comes before the noun phrase it predicates. گردان is a collective noun 'troop'; همه modifies it as 'all/the whole'; همگروه reinforces 'companions/fellow-members'. The verb governs the following noun phrase (verb–subject order is common in poetry).
segments.s064-g3: بدیدند is past, 3rd-person plural 'they saw'. مر here is the archaic/dialectal form 'to me' (indirect/locative 'toward me'), so the clause means they saw the champion coming to/at my place. پهلوان is the definite object 'the champion' marked by را; پگاه 'dawn' is an adverbial time expression placed after the object. The clause has a slightly compressed literary word order typical of Ferdowsi.
segments.s064-g4: وزان is a fused prepositional/adverbial 'and/from that' (و + زان) indicating source or consequence — 'from that place/then'. جایگه is 'place'; برگرفتند is past, 3rd-person plural 'they took up' or 'they set out (took up)'; راه is the direct object 'the road' (they 'took the road'). The phrase thus means 'from that place they took the road' with the normal verb–object order.
segments.s064-g5: سپهبد is the subject 'the spahbed (general)'. فرستاد is a past-tense verb 'sent/dispatched' (here with purposive force). خواننده را is the direct object marked by را ('the reciter/announcer'); S–V–O order is straightforward and typical of narrative.
segments.s064-g6: که introduces a subordinate clause ('who' / 'to the effect that') describing the reciter's action. خواند is past 3rd-person singular and here means 'called/summoned' (not merely 'read'). بزرگان داننده را is the object of خواند: بزرگان 'the great/important ones' modified by داننده 'the learned/experts'; را marks the definite object. The whole clause: 'who summoned the great learned men.'
segments.s064-g7: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. دستور فرزانه is a noun phrase 'the wise order/command' (dastur + postpositive adjective فرزانه 'wise'). با موبدان is an accompaniment/instrumental phrase 'with the mobeds (priests)'. The clause sets the scene for the order that follows.
segments.s064-g8: This is a compressed, formulaic utterance of the order. سرافراز is an adjective 'honored/triumphant' used predicatively/imperatively in context ('make ... honored'); گردان here is the noun 'troop' that is being described/affected. و links the two halves; فرخ is an adjective 'fortunate/auspicious'; ردان is a participial/agentive form meaning 'driving away' or 'expelling' (in the poem it functions as part of the terse command). The line uses elliptical, elevated diction common in epic commands — adjectives are applied to the nouns in a terse, causative/optative sense.
segments.s064-g49: به گیتی بماند = a clause meaning 'remain in the world'. به + گیتی gives the locative/scene 'in/within the world'; بماند is the 3rd-person singular present/subjunctive of ماندن used here as a prophetic/optative or narrative present meaning 'will remain / let remain'. ز فرزند نام is a genitive phrase 'a name from/of (his) descendants' (ز = poetic 'from/of'), placed after the verb for poetic word order.
segments.s064-g50: که introduces the explanatory clause 'for/that'. این پور زالست = 'this son is Zal's': پور = 'son/descendant'; زالست is Zāl + -ست (archaic/poetic copula, contraction of است), so the copula is attached to the name. و آن پور سام = 'and that son (is) Sam's' with the copula omitted (understood) after و.
segments.s064-g51: بدو گردَد آراسته تاج و تخت = 'an adorned crown and throne will be turned to him / will come to him'. بدو = به + او 'to him/unto him'; گردد is the 3rd-person singular present/subjunctive of گشتن used in the sense 'becomes/comes/turns'; آراسته is a past-participial adjective 'adorned' modifying تاج و تخت (crown and throne). The verb precedes the adjectives and nouns in poetic order.
segments.s064-g52: ازان رفته نام و بدین مانده بخت = 'from that (one) the name has gone, and to this (one) the fortune has remained.' ازان = 'from that/thereof' (archaic demonstrative); رفته is past participle 'gone/faded' modifying نام (name/reputation) so 'the name has gone'; و links the clauses; بدین is the contracted form meaning 'to this' (ب- as prefix 'to/into' + این) and مانده is past participle 'remained', with بخت = 'fortune/fate' as the thing that remains.
segments.s064-g53: کنون این همه داستان منست = 'Now all these tales are fitting/appropriate.' کنون = 'now'; این همه داستان = 'all these tales' (demonstrative + quantifier + noun); منست is the adjectival predicate from منس with the archaic copular -ست, meaning 'is well-arranged/appropriate'.
segments.s064-g54: گل و نرگس بوستان منست = 'the rose and the narcissus (are) a garden / make a garden and are fitting.' The two nouns گل و نرگس form the subject; بوستان is a nominal predicate 'garden'; منست again is 'is fitting/appropriate'. Poetic word order places the predicate after the subject without an explicit copula.
segments.s064-g55: که از من رمیدست صبر و خرد = 'for/from me patience and wisdom have fled.' که = 'for/since' giving the reason; از من = 'from me'; رمیدست is the compound verb meaning 'has fled/has run away' (past/resultative), here applied to the coordinated subject صبر و خرد (patience and wisdom). The verb appears after the prepositional phrase and before the subject in poetic order.
segments.s064-g56: بگویید کاین را چه اندر خورد = 'Tell (me), what has happened to this?' بگویید is an imperative/plural polite of گفتن 'say/tell (you plural / you polite)'; کاین = 'that/this' (demonstrative); را is the definite object/topic marker attached to کاین; چه = 'what'; اندر خورد is a fixed phrase 'to befall/occur' (اندر = 'in/onto', خورد = here 'befall/happen' rather than 'eat'), so the clause asks 'what befalls/what is the matter with this?'.
segments.s064-g41: 'و دیگر که' is a connective phrase: 'و' (and) + 'دیگر' (further/another) + the relative/particle 'که' introducing the clause that follows. 'مایه ز دین خدای' is a nominal phrase: 'مایه' (a source/means) governed by the poetic preposition 'ز' (from), with 'دین خدای' = 'God's law/ordinance' as the source.
segments.s064-g42: Main clause with negated perception verb: 'ندیدم' = 'I have not seen'. The following 'که' introduces what was not seen. 'ماندی' here = 'be left / remain' (past/optative sense used narratively). 'جوان را' = 'a young man' with object/definite marker 'را'. 'بجای' = 'in place (of)', so the clause means 'I have not seen that a young man remain in (or as) a place/replacement.'
segments.s064-g43: 'بویژه' = 'especially/notably' frames the comment; 'که باشد' is a subordinating clause ('that/when he is'). 'ز تخم بزرگ' = 'from/of a great seed/stock' — 'تخم' here = origin/lineage, 'بزرگ' = great.
segments.s064-g44: 'چو' here introduces a conditional ('if/when'). 'بی‌جفت باشد' = 'if he is without a match/unpaired.' 'بماند' = 'remains' (present/persistent sense). 'سترگ' is a poetic noun here meaning a notable sight/gaze/presence that endures; it is the thing that 'remains.'
segments.s064-g45: 'چه نیکوتر' is an exclamatory comparative: 'how much better.' 'از' functions as the comparative marker ('than/of'), and 'پهلوان جوان' is the noun phrase 'a young champion/hero' (pahlavān described by 'javan').
segments.s064-g46: 'که' = 'who/that' introduces the relative clause. 'گردد' = 'become' / 'is turned into' (here: his quality or fame continues). 'به فرزند' = 'into/for a descendant' (the preposition 'به' has the sense 'into/unto' here). 'روشن‌روان' = an adjectival epithet 'bright‑soul' describing the descendant — i.e., the hero's virtues live on in a cheerful/bright‑souled child.
segments.s064-g47: 'چو' = 'when.' 'هنگام رفتن' = 'the time of going' where 'رفتن' is used euphemistically for dying. 'فراز آیدش' literally 'the summons/what is on high comes to him' — idiomatically: the moment of departure (death) comes upon him; 'آیدش' = 'comes to him.'
segments.s064-g48: 'به فرزند نو' = 'to/for a newborn child' (the preposition 'به' = 'to/for', 'نو' = 'new'). 'روز بازآیدش' = 'his day returns to him' — here 'روز' = 'day/fortune/reputation' and 'بازآیدش' = 'is restored/returns (to someone)', so the line says the man's good fortune or renown is carried on by his newborn child.
segments.s064-g9: "به شادی" is an adverbial prepositional phrase meaning 'in/with joy' placed before the verb; "بر پهلوان" is a prepositional phrase 'to/onto the champion' marking the goal; "آمدند" is the past simple 3rd person plural 'they came'. The agent is plural (they) and is continued in the next segment.
segments.s064-g10: Two coordinated nominals "خردمند و روشن‌روان" ('the wise and the bright‑souled') form the subject; the plural verb "آمدند" repeats (or continues) the action 'they came'. The conjunction "و" simply links the two descriptors.
segments.s064-g11: "زبان تیز" is a metaphoric noun phrase 'a sharp tongue' (biting speech); "بگشاد" is an archaising past form 'opened/released' (here 'let loose' or 'spoke sharply') in 3rd‑person; "دستان سام" follows as a possessive phrase 'Sam's hands' (Sam is the implied subject of the previous verb). Word order is poetic/inverted: the verb "بگشاد" governs the preceding noun phrase.
segments.s064-g12: This is a sequence of descriptive noun phrases about Sam with an omitted copula: "لبی پر ز خنده" = 'a lip/mouth full of laughter' ("پر ز" = 'full of'), and "دلی شادکام" = 'a heart joyous/fortunate'. These are attributive, characterizing Sam's mood; the clauses are paratactic (juxtaposed) as is common in epic Persian.
segments.s064-g13: "نخست" is an adverb 'first/at first'; "آفرین" here is an exclamation/noun 'praise, acclamation' (object of the verb); "جهاندار" ('the sovereign') specifies whom the praise is for; "کرد" is the past 3rd‑person singular 'he did/uttered' forming the verbal phrase 'آفرین ... کرد' = 'he voiced praise'.
segments.s064-g14: "دل موبد" = 'the mōbed's heart' (object, possessive); "از خواب" = 'from sleep' (prepositional phrase); "بیدار" is an adjective 'awake'; "کرد" is causative/past 'he made' so together 'he made the mōbed's heart awake' = 'he roused the priest's heart (from sleep)'.
segments.s064-g15: "چنین گفت" = 'he said thus' (quotative opener); "کز" introduces the subordinate clause meaning 'that / since / because'; "داور" is the judge/arbiter (here likely the divine or just judge); "راد و پاک" are coordinating adjectives 'noble/generous and pure' describing that judge. The clause introduced by کز will give the reason in the next segment.
segments.s064-g16: "دل ما" = 'our heart' (possessed noun); "پر" = 'full (of)'; "امید و ترس" = 'hope and fear' (two nouns coordinated); "است" = copula 'is'; and final "و باک" adds 'and apprehension/dread' as an additional noun in apposition. The whole is a predicative clause stating the state of the heart.
segments.s064-g217: 'سخن هر چه ... دشوارتر' uses the correlative comparative construction 'هر چه ... تر' = 'the more...'. Here 'سخن' (subject) + 'هر چه' introduce the standard 'the more X is...' pattern; 'بر بنده' is a prepositional phrase with 'بر' meaning 'for/toward' marking the experiencer ('for the servant'), and 'دشوارتر' is the comparative adjective 'more difficult' agreeing with 'سخن'. Word order is adjective after the modifying phrase, typical in classical Persian.
segments.s064-g218: This clause gives the correlative result: 'دلش خسته‌تر' = 'his heart is more tired.' The enclitic 'ش' attaches to 'دل' to form 'his heart.' 'زان' links back to the previous clause and functions as 'for that reason/therefore.' The conjunction 'و' connects the parallel predicate about the body ('تن زارتر' = 'his body more wretched'); adjectives are comparative and follow the nouns they modify.
segments.s064-g219: 'گشاده‌تر آن باشد اندر نهان' is a predicative clause where the comparative adjective 'گشاده‌تر' ('more open/relieved') is placed before the demonstrative 'آن' ('that'). 'باشد' is the copular verb (classical subjunctive/present form often used to state a general truth, here 'will/is'), and 'اندر نهان' is a locative phrase 'in secret/within.' The overall order (adjective — demonstrative — copula — locative) reflects classical poetic word order.
segments.s064-g220: 'چو' here is the temporal conjunction 'when.' The noun 'فرمان' ('decree/command') is the thing given, and 'دهد' is the verb 'gives' (third singular). 'کردگار جهان' ('Maker/Creator of the world') names the agent; the phrase literally reads 'when the Creator of the world gives a decree.' Classical Persian allows this relatively free order (temporal particle + object + verb + agent).
segments.s064-g121: فزاینده is an active participle meaning "increasing/strengthening" and precedes the noun it modifies (باد). باد here is the literal noun "wind" (not the optative particle). آوردگاه (battlefield) follows in apposition: the phrase reads as a nominal group "the strengthening wind (of) the battlefield" or more naturally "the strengthening wind that swept the battlefield."
segments.s064-g122: فشاننده is another active participle, "sprinkling/ scattering," describing the action of the wind. خون is the direct object "blood." ز is the poetic preposition "from," introducing the source; ابر سیاه = "black cloud," with سیاه qualifying ابر. The whole segment literally: "sprinkling blood from the black cloud."
segments.s064-g123: گراینده is an active participle meaning "one who directs/causes" or "bestows/appoints," used here as an agentive modifier. تاج "crown" and زرین کمر "golden girdle/belt" are the items associated with that act; زرین is the adjective "golden" modifying کمر. The word order lists the bestowed items after the agentive participle.
segments.s064-g124: نشاننده is used as an active participle "one who sets/marks/places." زال is the proper name (the legendary hero Zāl) and is the patient of نشاننده. بر (here) means "on/upon," تخت is "throne," and زر is "gold," so بر تخت زر = "on the golden throne." The segment describes the act of installing/placing Zāl on the golden throne.
segments.s064-g125: به marks the beneficiary or target and here introduces the phrase "to/for a man": به مردی = "to/for a man." هنر در هنر is a poetic pluralizing/intensifying construction literally "skill within skill" (i.e., skill upon skill or excellence in every art). ساخته is a past participle/adjective "made/constructed," so the phrase means "(he) made/formed a man skilled in art upon art." Word order places the beneficiary and descriptive phrase before the verb-like participle.
segments.s064-g126: خرد is the nominal "wisdom." از + هنرها can indicate source or comparison — here it reads as "from/of the arts" or more idiomatically "above the arts." برافراخته is a predicate adjective/participle meaning "raised aloft/lofty." Together: "wisdom, raised high from (or above) the arts."
segments.s064-g127: من is the subject "I." او را uses را as an object/topic marker with او to indicate the person concerned ("toward him/regarding him"). بسان = "like/as" introduces the simile. یکی here means "one/a single" ("one servant") and بنده‌ام is the first-person form of بنده "servant;" the -ام ending marks the copula "I am." The phrase literally: "I am to him like one servant."
segments.s064-g128: به مهرش = "to/for/by his love" (به marks the relation, مهرش = his love/affection). روان and دل are two coordinated nouns ("spirit/soul and heart") that share the predicate آگنده‌ام, where آگنده‌ام is a stative participle + 1st-person suffix meaning "I am filled/filled up." The line thus says the speaker's soul is given to his love and his heart is full.
segments.s064-g57: نگفتم is a past-tense negative verb 'I did not say' placed before the pronoun من (a common poetic inversion). این is the direct object 'this'. تا introduces the temporal clause 'until' and نگشتم is the past of گشت meaning 'I became/turned (into)', followed by غمی 'grief' as the state that seized the speaker. Altogether: 'I did not say this until I became grief-stricken.'
segments.s064-g58: به ... در نیامد is the construction 'did not come into' (به + noun + در آمدن = to enter into). مغز و خرد are two coordinated nouns 'brain and wisdom/mind'. کمی 'a little' follows the negated verb نیامد and with the negation here functions as 'not even a little' (i.e. none at all).
segments.s064-g59: همه modifies کاخ ('the whole palace'). مهراب مهر is a poetic noun–noun apposition 'mihrab (sanctuary) of love' (mɛhrاب = niche/mihrab, مهر = love). منست is a predicate meaning 'is suitable/fitting' (an archaic/formal copular/adjectival predicate).
segments.s064-g60: زمینش is زمین + enclitic ش 'its/its ground'. چو introduces a simile 'like/as'. گردان is an adjectival/participial form 'revolving' modifying سپهر 'sky/heaven'. Again منست is the predicative 'is fitting/appropriate'.
segments.s064-g61: دلم combines دل 'heart' with the possessive enclitic -م 'my'. گشت is past 'became/turned'. با marks the agent or accompanying cause 'by/with' and is followed by دخت سیندخت 'the maiden Sindukht' (noun + proper name). رام is predicate/adjective 'tame/gentle' describing the new state of the heart.
segments.s064-g62: چه begins an exclamatory/rhetorical question 'what/who'. گوینده is 'speaker' or 'narrator'. باشد is the present/subjunctive copula here meaning 'would there be' / 'could there be'. بدین is the compound prepositional notion 'to/for this' (ب 'to' + دین = this) and رام سام 'tame Sam' is the indirect object of the rhetorical question — 'who could be a speaker to this tame Sam?' (i.e. who could address Sam under these circumstances).
segments.s064-g63: شود is the present/future/conditional auxiliary 'becomes/should it become'. رام is the adjective 'tame/mild'. گویی here is parenthetical and functions like 'one might say' or 'it might be said' (a rhetorical comment); it interrupts the clause before the subject. منوچهر شاه is the subject phrase 'Manuchehr the king' that follows (classical inversion).
segments.s064-g64: جوانی 'youth/the young' is the subject/agent implied for the following action. گمانی برد is an idiomatic verbal phrase 'to take as a suspicion/assume as a conjecture' (گمانی = supposition; برد = 'takes'/'harbors'). یا links alternatives 'or', and گناه presents the alternative reading 'sin' — i.e. 'will they take it as presumption or as sin?'
segments.s064-g17: This phrase places the prepositional phrase 'به بخشایش' before the noun 'امید' for poetic emphasis: literally 'to/for forgiveness — hope'. The coordinate 'امید و ترس' links 'hope' and 'fear', and 'از گناه' is a following prepositional phrase modifying 'ترس' (= 'fear of sin'). 'را' is absent because these are not marked as definite objects here.
segments.s064-g18: 'به فرمانها' is a directional/target prepositional phrase ('to the commandments'). 'ژرف کردن' is a light-verb construction in which the adjective 'ژرف' ('deep/profound') combines with the verb 'کردن' to mean 'to deepen' (i.e. 'to make deep'). 'نگاه' is the noun 'gaze' functioning as the object of that verbal idea: literally 'to make the gaze deep toward the commandments' = 'look deeply upon the commandments.'
segments.s064-g19: The infinitive 'ستودن' ('to praise') begins the clause; 'مراو را' is the definite object (the person praised), with the object marker 'را' attached. The sequence 'چنان چون توان' is an idiomatic comparative: 'چنان... چون' = 'such as, to the extent that' and 'توان' (ability) supplies 'as one can' — i.e. 'praise him as much as one is able.'
segments.s064-g20: 'شب و روز بودن' is a nominal/verbal phrase 'to be night and day' meaning 'to be continually' or 'to remain day and night'. 'به پیشش' is a prepositional phrase 'in his presence' (پیش + pronominal clitic -ش). 'نوان' is a predicate adjective ('youthful/new') placed after the infinitival predicate 'بودن', so the phrase reads 'to be before him night and day (and) youthful.'
segments.s064-g21: Noun phrase 'خداوند گردنده خورشید و ماه' = 'the Lord, the one who turns/sets the sun and moon in motion.' 'گردنده' is an agentive/present-participial adjective modifying 'خداوند'; 'خورشید و ماه' are the coordinated objects of that verbal idea.
segments.s064-g22: 'روان را' is the definite object ('the soul' marked by را). 'به نیکی' is a manner/adverbial phrase 'with goodness' or 'to goodness'. 'نماینده راه' functions as a verb-like nominal phrase: 'نماینده' here carries the sense of 'making (something) manifest / showing' and 'راه' is the thing shown — together yielding 'show the way' or 'make the path apparent.' The construction is poetic/nominalized rather than using a finite verb.
segments.s064-g23: 'بدویست' is an archaic/poetic adjective placed before 'گیهان' to qualify the world (literally 'primordial' or 'of old') — here it modifies 'گیهان' ('the world'). 'خرم' is the predicate adjective 'joyful/green' describing the world, and 'به پای' is a prepositional phrase 'at the feet (of...)' whose possessor is supplied in the following segment.
segments.s064-g24: 'همو' is an emphatic pronoun 'he himself / the same one' supplying the possessor of 'به پای' from the previous segment. The coordinated nouns 'داد و داور' here mean 'justice and judge' (i.e. he is the source/giver of justice and the arbiter). 'به هر دو سرای' is a prepositional phrase 'in/to both abodes/realms' (classical poetic formula for 'both worlds' — this world and the next). Note: the lemma senses given for 'سرای' in the data do not include this 'abode/realm' sense, so that sense is used contextually.
segments.s064-g113: ازویست is a fused, somewhat archaic/poetic form meaning 'it is from him' (from + او + است). In this construction the verb+preposition phrase precedes the noun (inversion common in poetry): 'ازویست شادی' = 'joy is from him' (literally 'from-him-it-is joy'). The second ازویست repeats the same copular idea, linking to زور in the same way.
segments.s064-g114: This noun phrase names the referent introduced by the previous clauses: 'خداوند' here functions as 'lord/sovereign' (an honorific title). The following three words are a simple coordinated list of divine/heavenly names ('کیوان' = Kēyvān/Saturn, 'ناهید' = Nāhid/Venus, 'هور' = Hūr/'splendor' or a light-deity). The conjunction و is the regular 'and.'
segments.s064-g115: خداوند هست is the plain copula/existential clause 'God exists' (خداوند = God; هست = is/exists). The coordintion ... و خداوند نیست places the negative copula نیست ('is not/does not exist') parallel to هست; the paradoxical pairing is a poetic device but grammatically straightforward: subject + present copula / negative copula.
segments.s064-g116: همه بندگانیم is composed of همه ('all/everyone') + بندگانیم (بندگان + یم), where یم is the archaic/poetic first-person-plural copula 'we are' attached to the plural noun — 'we are all servants/worshipers.' The following و links to ایزد یکیست: ایزد ('the Deity, god') + یکیست (one + است) = 'is one.'
segments.s064-g117: ازو ('from him') + باد: باد here is the optative particle meaning 'may it be' (not 'wind'). The preposition بر ('upon') introduces the beneficiary 'سام' (Sam). نیرم is an epithet attached to Sam (postpositive adjective/epithet), and درود is the noun 'greeting/blessing' receiving the optative: 'May there be from him a blessing upon Sam Nīram.'
segments.s064-g118: This short phrase names items/persons associated with (or bestowed by) 'خداوند' (here 'the lord/sovereign'). The verb is omitted (a common poetic ellipsis): syntactically this can be read as an appositive or an elided clause meaning 'the lord — Kopāl and sword and himself' (کوپال is the name of a weapon, شمشیر = 'sword', خود = 'himself/own').
segments.s064-g119: چماننده is a simile/participle meaning 'resembling' or 'like.' It takes دیزه ('fortress') as its object. هنگام ('at the time') plus گرد ('around/about'; here marking the time of tumult/attack) form a temporal phrase 'at the time of the (surrounding) assault.' The whole phrase is a comparative clause: 'like a fortress at the time of (the) attack.'
segments.s064-g120: This parallel simile names the agent: چراننده is an active participle 'grazing/pasturing' used metaphorically for a predator 'feeding' and modifies کرگس ('vulture'). اندر is the locative/prepositional 'in/within' and نبرد is 'battle,' so 'a vulture feeding in the battle.' The two similes stand in parallel.
segments.s064-g65: The phrase 'چه مهتر چه کهتر' is a correlative 'چه ... چه ...' meaning 'whether ... or ...' (here 'greater or lesser'). 'چو شد' is an archaic/narrative 'when it became' (چو = when, شد = became). 'جفت جوی' is a compound idea: جفت (pair) + جوی (seeking / joining) meaning 'were joined/paired'. The whole segment means 'when (they) became paired'.
segments.s064-g66: سوی + دین و آیین gives the direction/goal: 'toward religion and ritual'. نهادست is a fused literary form (نهاد + است) meaning 'is placed / is turned' (here 'has set its face'); روی = 'face' (literally 'turned its face toward'). The clause says the (paired) turned toward religious observance.
segments.s064-g67: بدین در is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in this matter' (بدین = به این). خردمند را uses the object/topic marker را after 'the wise one' to mark the experiencer; جنگ نیست is the predicate 'there is no war/strife'. Together: 'In this matter, for the wise there is no conflict.'
segments.s064-g68: که introduces a explanatory clause 'for/that'. هم راه دینست: هم 'also/both', راه 'path/way', دینست = دین + است 'is of religion' meaning 'it accords with religion'. و هم ننگ نیست: 'and likewise there is no disgrace/shame.' The clause gives the reason why the wise have no quarrel.
segments.s064-g69: چه is the interrogative 'what'. گوید is a narrative/present tense 'says' (classical present used in narration). کنون 'now'. موبد پیش بین is an appositive noun phrase 'the mobed, the foreseer/seer' (پیش‌بین as a title). The clause asks what the priest-seer utters now.
segments.s064-g70: Another question: چه 'what' + دانید 'do (they) know' — here دانید is a classical 3rd-plural/present form 'know' referring to فرزانگان (the sages). اندرین = 'in this / about this'. The clause asks 'what do the sages know about this (matter)?'.
segments.s064-g71: ببستند is past plural 'they closed/shut'. لب موبدان و ردان is the direct object 'the lips of the mobeds and the readers/reciters' (رَدان here refers to those who recite/chant). The clause means 'they shut the mouths of the priests and reciters.'
segments.s064-g72: سخن بسته شد is a passive/resultative construction 'speech was closed/sealed'. بر لب بخردان = 'on the lips of the wise' (بخردان = the wise ones); so 'speech was sealed on the wise men's lips.' The verb شد (became) forms the passive/resultive state.
segments.s064-g105: منوچهر is the subject (the king Manuchehr). هم here means “also/too,” marking that Manuchehr likewise acted the same. رای سام is a genitive phrase “the counsel/opinion of Sam,” and سوار is an appositive to سام (“Sam, the rider”), placed after the name to describe him.
segments.s064-g106: نپردازد is a literary negative perfective/present (third singular) meaning here “did not depart/swerve”; از ره is a prepositional phrase “from the path/way” meaning “deviate from the course.” بدین مایه کار = “in/with this kind of matter” (بدین = به + این, مایه کار = this sort/means of affair) and specifies the manner; the whole verb phrase applies back to the subject ‘منوچهر.’ (This uses elevated/archaic diction.)
segments.s064-g107: سپهبد is the subject (“the spahbed/general”). نویسنده is the direct object and is marked by the object marker را. پیش خواند is a compound: پیش (= in the presence of / before) + خواند (here “to call/summon”), so the clause means “the spahbed summoned the scribe (before him).”
segments.s064-g108: دل آگنده بودش is an inverted clause meaning “his heart was filled/overflowing,” where بودش is the past copula with enclitic ش = “his.” همه برفشاند: همه (“all/altogether”) modifies برفشاند (past tense “he scattered/poured forth”); the object of برفشاند is left implicit in the poetic line. The order and enclitic are typical of classical Persian.
segments.s064-g109: یکی نامه فرمود نزدیک سام — 'یکی' is the indefinite subject ('someone' / 'a certain one') used classically to introduce an action. 'نامه فرمود' is a literary phrase meaning 'wrote/sent a letter' (فرمود here functions as a high-register verb of saying/commanding and is used metonymically for sending or issuing a letter). 'نزدیک سام' places the recipient/target: نزدیک here has the spatial/relational sense 'near/to' and with the name سام marks that the letter was addressed toward Sam; word order is Subject — Object — Verb with the directional phrase after the verb phrase in this line of verse.
segments.s064-g110: سراسر نوید و درود و خرام — 'سراسر' ('throughout, entire') modifies the whole following list, meaning the letter was wholly filled with what follows. 'نوید' and 'درود' are nouns ('good news' and 'greetings') joined by و, and 'خرام' is a nominalized notion of gait/attitude (a 'proud stride' or 'proud bearing'); the string is a coordinated list of contents/tones of the letter. The overall order is modifier + a coordinated noun phrase.
segments.s064-g111: ز خط نخست آفرین گسترید — 'ز' is the poetic/prepositional form 'from'. 'خط نخست' = 'the first line' (خط = 'line', نخست = 'first'). 'آفرین' is an acclamation/praise noun ('praise, acclaim') and 'گسترید' is a past-tense verb ('he spread' / 'was spread' depending on voice). The clause literally reads 'From the first line (object) praise he spread' (object before verb is normal in poetry).
segments.s064-g112: بدان دادگر کو جهان آفرید — here 'ب' is the prepositional element 'به' ('to/unto'); it combines with the following unit to mean 'to that'. The sequence names the addressee of the praise: 'دادگر' is an epithet meaning 'the just (ruler/judge)'. 'کو' is an archaic relative/particle introducing the relative clause 'who/that', and 'جهان آفرید' is the relative clause 'who created the world' with the verb آفرید ('created') at the end, as is standard in formal/classical word order.
segments.s064-g209: سوی + noun phrase marks direction or presence: 'سوی شهریاران' = 'toward / before the kings'. سر انجمن is a noun phrase 'head (سر) of the council/assembly (انجمن)'—سر here denotes the leader/head of that gathering. Word order is noun-modifier (سر + انجمن) forming a genitive-like compound 'head of the assembly'.
segments.s064-g210: شوم is the 1st-person subjunctive/potential 'may I become' (a common older/poetic form expressing possibility). خام گفتار is a compound: خام modifies گفتار meaning 'naive/simple in speech'. و connects the two predicates; پیمان‌شکن is a nominal adjective 'oath-breaker; faithless' describing the speaker if that happens.
segments.s064-g211: وگر is the conditional 'and if / but if'. گویم here is a verb 'I say' (1st-person present), though the supplied lemma senses are nominal and thus don't match the verbal use (see senses). آری is the interjection 'yes'. و links clauses; 'کامت رواست' is a copular clause: کامت (کام + possessive -ت) = 'your desire', رواست = 'is permitted / proper' (روا + است). The clause as a whole expresses a hypothetical assent.
segments.s064-g212: بپرداز is an imperative/hortative verb 'deal with / give (attention to) / follow'. دل را uses را as the definite/object marker: 'your heart' as direct object. بدانچت is an enclitic construction (ب + دانچت) meaning 'to that which you have' or 'to that which is your desire'—the supplied senses list lacks an entry so it's marked -1. هواست is a verblike predicate 'it is desire/whim' (هوا = desire; ‑است copula). The clause instructs: 'attend to your heart, since that is your whim/desire.'
segments.s064-g213: ازین is a fused preposition + demonstrative 'from this' (از + این). مرغ پرورده is a noun + participle 'the bird reared/nurtured'. وان is the demonstrative 'that'. دیوزاد is a compound 'demon-born' (دیـو + زاد) meaning a fiend or offspring of a demon; together the phrase lists two contrasting progenitors.
segments.s064-g214: چه here is an interrogative/exclamatory 'what/how' introducing a question. گویی in this line is the 2nd/3rd-person present of گفتن 'you say' (poetic form), but the supplied lemma senses are nominal and don't match the verb form (so marked -1). چگونه is 'how'. برآید is present/subjunctive 'would arise / come into being'. نژاد = 'lineage / race / breed'. The string questions the possibility of such a lineage arising from those two parents.
segments.s064-g215: سرش = سر + possessive suffix 'his head'. گشت is the past/preterite or resultative 'became / was turned' here indicating a change of state. از اندیشه = 'from/through thought' (از marks cause). دل گران = 'his heart heavy' (دل + adjective گران 'heavy, burdened'). The clause describes a mental change caused by thinking.
segments.s064-g216: بخفت is an older/poetic verb form from خفتن (to sleep/sink/collapse); contextually here it means 'sank down / was overcome' (not merely literal sleep). و is 'and'. نیاسوده is a participial/adjectival compound meaning 'not at rest / uneasy / restless'. گشت is again 'became'. اندران = اندر + ان 'in/among them' = 'among them / therein'. The clause says he became restless among the company.
segments.s064-g73: که is the subordinating particle 'that' introducing a clause. The noun ضحاک (Zahhāk) is the subject; مهراب را uses را as the definite/object marker directly after مهراب to mark it as the object. بد is an adjective 'evil/ill' modifying the following element; نیا here is an archaic/poetic element whose exact lexical sense is uncertain in this context (see senses). The sequence therefore reads roughly 'that Zahhāk did evil to the mihrāb/mihrāb was ill-treated by Zahhāk.'
segments.s064-g74: دل شاه is a possessive phrase 'the king's heart' (noun followed by possessor). ازیشان = از + ایشان 'because of / on account of them' gives the reason; پر از is the adjectival phrase 'full of' (پر followed by the preposition از + noun), and کیمیا here means 'the elixir / secret (metaphorical: a crafty/corrupting influence)'. Word order is typical Persian: possessor phrase then reason phrase then predicate 'full of X.'
segments.s064-g75: گشاده سخن is a noun phrase 'open speech' (adjective before noun). کس as subject means 'anyone / no one' in this negative-context line. نیارست is a negative/past inability ('could not') in archaic/poetic register and is followed by گفت 'said' — together conveying 'no one could speak out, (so) he said...'. The verb-expression is somewhat inverted for poetic effect.
segments.s064-g76: که introduces the reported clause 'that …'. نشنید is past tense 'did not hear' (or 'had not heard'); کس again 'anyone/no one' as object of hearing. نوش here is 'delight/pleasant draught' and با نیش جفت means 'paired with a sting' (با = 'with', نیش = 'sting/insult', جفت = 'paired/matched'). The clause means 'that no one had heard delight joined with sting.'
segments.s064-g77: چو = 'when' (temporal conjunction) introducing a subordinate clause. نشنید 'did not hear' is the verb; از ایشان 'from them' gives the source; سپهبد سخن places the title 'spahbed' and the noun 'speech/word' together — in Persian the order can be flexible, so the clause means 'when the spahbed heard no word from them.' The subject/title (سپهبد) and object (سخن) are arranged for poetic word order but read naturally as 'the spahbed (subject) heard no speech from them.'
segments.s064-g78: بجوشید is a past tense verb meaning 'boiled' or 'seethed' (here of speech or feeling). و 'and' links the next clause. رای نو is 'a new counsel/plan' (رای = counsel/plan, نو = new) and افگند 'cast/threw' is the past verb that follows; بن 'bin/ban' here is 'root/foundation' or can be an adverbial/poetic particle completing the image—overall 'his words boiled up and he cast a new counsel (forth).' The action verbs are past tense and describe sudden emotional and deliberative response.
segments.s064-g79: The first که introduces direct speech or a clause complement; دانم is the speaker's present 'I know'. The following که again introduces the subordinate content clause. چون این پژوهش کنید is a temporal/subordinating phrase: چون here functions like 'when/if' and پژوهش کنید is 2nd-person plural subjunctive/imperative used proverbially for 'when you examine/do this' (کنید = 'you do' / 'you will do'). The whole segment is 'I know that when you examine this...'.
segments.s064-g80: بدین رای is a prepositional phrase 'by/with this counsel' (بدین = 'by this / with this', رای = 'counsel/plan'). بر من is the prepositional object 'against me / onto me' and نکوهش کنید is the 2nd-person plural present/subjunctive 'you reproach' (used here as an outcome: 'you will reproach'). The order is typical Persian: prepositional phrase of cause, then target, then verb 'you will reproach me (for) this counsel.'
segments.s064-g81: ولیکن is an adversative conjunction 'but'. هر آنکو is an indefinite relative 'whoever/whosoever' (هر + آنکو) functioning as the subject. بود is the past/copular verb 'was' (here a full past tense verb). پر + منش is an adjectival phrase where پر = 'full (of)' modifies منش 'character/disposition' (so 'full of disposition' = 'proud by nature'). Word order places the relative subject before the verb, with the adjectival phrase after it.
segments.s064-g82: بباید is a modal impersonal form 'must/ought (it is necessary)'. It takes the verbal noun/infinitive شنیدن 'to hear' after it, forming 'must hear'. بسی modifies the noun سرزنش 'reproach' and means 'much' / 'many' in this poetic context. The construction is impersonal: '[he] must hear much reproach.'
segments.s064-g83: مرا (me + object marker را split here) is the direct object 'me'. اندرین‌گر is a conditional/adverbial phrase 'in this case / if so', introducing the manner. نمایش کنید is an imperative plural 'show / present' (نمایش = 'display/presentation', کنید = 'do' in the polite/plural imperative). The object precedes the verb; the conditional adverb modifies the whole command: 'Show me in this way.' Note: the segmentation in the word list separates م and را; functionally they form 'مرا'.
segments.s064-g84: وزین is an adjective 'weighty / dignified / grave' qualifying بند 'stanza/verse' (here 'this weighty stanza'). راه گشایش کنید is an imperative phrase 'open the way' or 'open a path' (راه = 'way', گشایش = 'opening', کنید = imperative 'do'). The adjective + noun phrase precedes the verb phrase: 'Open the way with/by this weighty stanza.'
segments.s064-g85: به جای شما is a prepositional phrase 'in your place / instead of you' (به + جای = 'in the place of'); شما is the second-person object. آن is the demonstrative object 'that', placed before the verb. کنم is the first-person singular present/subjunctive form used here with future/intentive force 'I will do'. در جهان is a locative adverbial 'in the world', placed after the verb phrase. Note the subject is incorporated in the verb ending -م.
segments.s064-g86: که introduces a relative clause 'that/which' modifying the prior آن. با + کهتران means 'toward/with the lesser ones' (کهتران = 'the lesser/lowly'); کس here in a negative context means 'no one' (كس ... نكرد = 'no one did'), and نکرد is the past negative 'did not do' (ن- prefix + کرد). از مهان is a partitive/agent phrase 'of/from the great (men)' meaning 'among the great' — the clause reads 'which no one among the great did to the lesser.'
segments.s064-g87: This is a poetic list introduced by the preposition ز/از: ز خوبی و از نیکی و راستی = 'in/from goodness and from virtue and honesty'. The repeated ز/از is a classical rhetorical device enumerating qualities. Each noun (خوبی, نیکی, راستی) names a moral quality; راستی here reads as 'uprightness/honesty'.
segments.s064-g88: ز بد here functions 'because of/from badness'; ناورم is a negated verbal form (classical/poetic) meaning 'I do not bring' or 'I am not the bearer of' (the lemma given in the list does not match this usage exactly, so the precise lexical item is archaic). بر شما is the prepositional phrase 'toward you / upon you' and کاستی is the noun 'deficiency/shortcoming'. Together: 'I will not bring to you any deficiency because of evil' i.e. 'I bear you no lack on account of badness.'
segments.s064-g201: چنین داد پاسخ = 'he gave an answer thus/so'. Such/so (چنین) precedes the verb for emphasis; داد is the past-tense verb 'gave/said' and پاسخ is its direct object. که introduces a subordinate clause; آمد پدید is a poetic inversion where آمد (came/happened) combines with پدید (appear) to mean 'came forth/appeared'.
segments.s064-g202: سخن هر چه از گوهر بد سزید = 'every saying/word that came from an evil essence deserved (it)'. هر چه = 'whatever/whichever' introduces the relative idea; سزید is past tense of سزا 'to deserve' (here 'deserved'). از marks source ('from'), and گوهر بد is a genitive-like descriptive phrase 'evil essence/core.'
segments.s064-g203: چو مرغ ژیان باشد آموزگار = 'when/as the living bird is a teacher'. چو here introduces a conditional/temporal clause ('when'/'if'). مرغ ژیان is a noun phrase ('bird of life' / 'living bird') and باشد is the literary present/subjunctive 'is' linking to the predicate noun آموزگار ('teacher').
segments.s064-g204: چنین کام دل جوید از روزگار = 'the heart seeks such a desire from fate'. چنین modifies کام ('such a wish/fulfilment'); دل is the subject/agent ('the heart'); جوید is present/habitual 'seeks' (classical present form); از روزگار indicates source ('from fate / from the times').
segments.s064-g205: ز نخچیر کامد سوی خانه باز = 'from the spoil (of the hunt) he came back toward the house'. ز marks origin ('from'); نخچیر means 'prey/spoil'; کامد (archaic literary form of آمد) = 'came/arrived'; سوی ... باز indicates direction 'toward the house back' — here باز functions as 'back/returned'. Word order is somewhat inverted for poetic effect.
segments.s064-g206: به دلش اندر اندیشه آمد دراز = 'a long thought came into his heart'. به دلش اندر = 'into his heart' (به ... اندر is a doubled locative for emphasis); اندیشه آمد = 'thought came' (subject-predicate order common in poetry), and دراز is a postpositive adjective 'long' qualifying اندیشه.
segments.s064-g207: همی گفت اگر گویم این نیست رای = 'he repeatedly/continually said: if I say this, it is not (my) opinion.' همی before a past verb often gives a continuous or habitual sense ('kept saying'). اگر گویم is a conditional subordinate clause ('if I say'), این is the content 'this', and نیست رای states 'it is not (a) judgment/opinion.'
segments.s064-g208: مکن داوری سوی دانش گرای = 'do not make/judge; turn toward the learned.' مکن is the negative imperative 'do not'; داوری is the noun 'judgment' (object of the negation: 'do not judge/pass judgment'); سوی + noun gives direction 'toward'; دانش گرای here points to knowledge/learned people — an exhortation to consult or incline toward the learned (the exact suffix/spacing is archaic/poetic but the sense is 'the learned').
segments.s064-g161: ز here is the poetic preposition 'from' (more naturally: 'away from'); پیمان = 'pledge/oath'. نگردد is an archaic/poetic negative third‑person form of گشت/گردیدن (here 'turn, depart'), so 'does not turn away'. سپهبد پدر is the title + apposition ('the spahbed, the father') and functions as the subject. Word order places the prepositional/negative phrase before the subject for emphasis.
segments.s064-g162: بدین کار = به + این contracted, 'in/for this matter' (an adverbial/prepositional phrase). دستور باشد مگر = 'there be an order, unless' — باشد is the optative/present subjunctive (literary) meaning 'there be/is', دستور is the noun 'order/command', and مگر introduces the exception 'unless'. Overall: 'in this matter there will be no turning away, unless there is an order.'
segments.s064-g163: که introduces a subordinate clause 'that'. من is the explicit subject 'I'. دخت مهراب را is the definite direct object ('Mehrab's daughter' + object marker را). جفت خویش is a noun phrase 'j pair/mate + خویش (reflexive/possessive)' = 'my own mate'; note the suffix ش marks possessive/reflexive element that agrees with the subject in this construction, and the verb is postponed to the next segment.
segments.s064-g164: کنم is the 1st‑person present/subjunctive form of کردن used here for a future/optative sense 'I will make/do'. راستی را is the object 'truth/honour' (here adverbial/ethical sense 'honestly/uprightly') and takes the object marker را. به آیین و کیش is a prepositional phrase 'according to ritual and creed' that qualifies the verb (how the action will be done).
segments.s064-g165: به پیمان 'by/under the pledge' is an adverbial phrase ('in accordance with the oath'). چنین 'thus/so' modifies the verb. رفت is simple past 'he went'. پیش گروه 'before/in front of the troop' (پیش + گروه) is a locative/complement indicating where he went; the implied subject is the same person referred to earlier.
segments.s064-g166: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. باز آوریدم is the poetic/aorist form 'I bring back / I will bring back' (آوریدم = 1st‑person form used in subordinate temporal clauses), with باز meaning 'back'. ز البرز کوه = 'from Mount Alborz' (ز = 'from', البرز کوه = the Alborz mountain). The clause sets a temporal/conditional frame 'when I bring him back from Alborz...'.
segments.s064-g167: که introduces purpose/result 'so that'. هیچ آرزو = 'no desire/none of your longings'. بر دلت = 'upon your heart' (بر + دل with the 2nd‑person suffix ت = 'your'). نگسلم is a negative present/subjunctive/optative form of گسستن 'to break, to part' with the 1st‑person ending -م; here idiomatically 'that I do not tear away/that will not part (from)'. The clause intends 'so that I may not cause any longing to remain/attach to your heart' (poetic nuance).
segments.s064-g168: کنون 'now' sets the present time. اندرین = 'in this / in these (circumstances)' (a literary locative). است 'is' (copula). بسته is a predicative adjective 'closed / bound / tied up' describing the state of the heart. دلم = دل + ـم 'my heart' (دل = 'heart', ـم = 1st‑person possessive suffix). Together: 'now my heart is bound in this.'
segments.s064-g145: گرفتار is used adjectivally here meaning 'subject to' or 'caught by'; it is followed by the genitive phrase فرمان یزدان (the command of God). The copula بود (past 'was') completes the predication: 'was subject to God's command.' Word order in older Persian places the predicate/adjectival گرفتار before the possessed phrase.
segments.s064-g146: وگر is a concessive/conditional conjunction ('but/though' or 'even if'). چند qualifies دندان as 'many/several' and the enclitic -ش on دندانش marks possession ('his/its teeth'). سندان بود is the verbless predicate with copula بود: 'were anvils.' The clause is concessive to the previous clause.
segments.s064-g147: یکی کار پیش آمدم is an idiomatic past expression: 'a certain affair came to pass to me' (i.e. 'happened to me'). یکی = 'a certain', کار = 'affair', پیش here = 'before/in the presence of' as part of the phrasal idea 'پیش آمد' = 'occurred', and آمدم is first-person past 'came/happened to me.' دل شکن is an appositive epithet 'heart-breaker' placed after the noun it qualifies.
segments.s064-g148: که introduces a relative clause 'which/that'. نتوان is the negative modal 'cannot' and is followed by the verb stem ستودن in the form ستودنش (with the object enclitic -ش 'it/him') meaning 'praise it.' بر انجمن ('upon/at the assembly') indicates the public setting — literal 'before the assembly' — and completes the thought: 'which I cannot praise in the assembly.'
segments.s064-g149: پدر‌گر should be read as 'پدر گر' where گر is the conditional particle 'if' attached to پدر ('if the father ...'). دلیرست and نراژدهاست are two predicates joined by و; each ends in the archaic copula -ست (classical form of است) meaning 'is.' The second predicate uses a poetic/compound term نراژدهاست meaning 'is fearsome / like a male-dragon.'
segments.s064-g150: اگر is the conditional 'if.' بشنود is the (subjunctive/optative or simple present) form of 'to hear' ('if he hears'). راز بنده is a genitive ('the secret of the bende') where بنده is the poet's humble self-reference ('this servant / me'). رواست is the predicate: روا = 'permissible/fitting' plus the copula ست, giving 'it is permissible/fitting.'
segments.s064-g151: من is the first-person subject 'I.' از introduces the cause/reason here ('because of' or 'on account of'). دخت مهراب is a genitive phrase 'the daughter of Mehrab' (دخت = daughter/maiden, مهراب here a name/title). گریان شدم is a compound past: گریان (weeping) + شدم (I became) = 'I began to weep / I became weeping.'
segments.s064-g152: چو is a subordinating conjunction meaning 'when/as.' بر آتش = 'upon the fire' (بر = on/upon), تیز modifies آتش as 'keen/sharp' and gives the intensity of the fire, and بریان شدم is the past expression 'I was roasted/burnt' (بریان = roasted/burnt/consumed, شدم = became). Together this gives a temporal result clause: 'when I was roasted on the keen fire, I became (consumed).'
segments.s064-g193: سپهدار (implied subject) is omitted here; بپرسید is a past-tense action 'he asked/inquired' formed with the preverb ب- + پرسید (poetic/older past). و is a simple connective 'and'. بستد is an older/polite past meaning 'closed, sealed' (here: sealed the letter). ازو + نامه forms a prepositional phrase 'the letter from/of him' (ازو = 'from him/of him'), with سام placed after نامه as the name of the letter's author ('Sam's letter').
segments.s064-g194: فرستاده is the agent 'the messenger'. گفت appears here as the main verb 'said', but the provided sense list lacks an explicit 'said' sense (so marked -1). آنچه بود is a relative clause 'that which was' (بود = 'was' in past). از پیام links that clause to its source: 'of/from the message' (i.e. 'what was in the message').
segments.s064-g195: سپهدار is the subject 'the commander'. بگشاد here functions as a past action 'he opened' (poetic/archaic formation with ب-). از نامه بند literally 'from the letter's bond/fastening' = 'the seal/fastening of the letter' (از + noun indicates removing or opening something from the letter).
segments.s064-g196: فرود آمد is a verbal phrase 'he descended' (فرود = descent/fall, آمد = came) used as a past action. از introduces the source/direction 'from'. تیغ کوه بلند is a poetic expression: تیغ (here as 'ridge/point') + کوه بلند 'of the tall mountain' = 'the high mountain's ridge/edge' (he came down from that ridge).
segments.s064-g197: سخنهای دستان is a genitive/attributive phrase: سخنهای 'the words/sayings' qualified by دستان 'of the tales/stories' (dastān as 'tales'). سراسر is an adverb/adjective 'throughout/entire' modifying the following verb. بخواند is an old/poetic past 'he read/recited' (ب- + خواند).
segments.s064-g198: بپژمرد is a past/result verb 'he withered/was overcome' (poetic b- prefix). و simply links the following clause. بر جای is a locative phrase 'in/at his place'; خیره is an adjective 'staring/astonished'; بماند is a past-tense form 'he remained' (b- + ماند) giving 'he remained staring in place'. Together they describe him standing fixed and stunned.
segments.s064-g199: پسندش نیامد is an idiomatic negation 'it did not please him' (پسند + object pronoun -ش, نیامد used idiomatically 'did not come' → 'did not please/was not fitting'). چنان آرزوی means 'such a wish' (چنان = 'such', آرزوی = 'wish'). The clause states that the wish was displeasing.
segments.s064-g200: دگرگونه 'different/otherwise' modifies the following predicate. بایستش here means 'it is/was fitting/necessary' or 'he judged it so' (archaic/modal sense 'ought/decided'). او را is the object 'him' (او + object marker را). به خوی 'according to his disposition/custom' (به = 'to/according to', خوی = 'disposition'). Together: 'He deemed it fitting to treat him otherwise, according to his nature.'
segments.s064-g137: همی is an iterative/adverbial particle meaning 'continually/kept on'. خواندندی is an archaic/poetic plural past form (comparable to خواندند) meaning 'they called' or 'they used to call'. مرا (here split in the word list as م + را) is the object 'me' with را marking definiteness/objecthood. پور سام is the phrase being used to name the speaker ('Sam's son') and functions as the predicative name in direct speech.
segments.s064-g138: به marks a goal/purpose ('to/for'); اورنگ بر سام is a prepositional/phrasing meaning 'the throne (for) Sam' or 'to set Sam on the throne' where بر is the motion/position preposition 'on/upon'. و is the coordinating 'and/while' linking the following clause; من is the subject 'I'; در کنام is a locative phrase 'in (his) lap' (کنام = bosom/lap). The clause contrasts the public naming/placement with the speaker's personal position.
segments.s064-g139: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; یزدان is the subject 'God' (divine power); چنین is an adverb 'thus/so'; راند is a past-tense verb 'drove/forced' (third person); اندر is the locative preposition 'in/into'; بوش here reads as a proper name or place (poetic/archaic), so the clause means 'when God thus drove [him] into Boush.'
segments.s064-g140: بران is a deictic/adverbial 'thereon/thereupon'; بود is the past copula 'was' (here introducing the resulting state). چرخ روان را روش is a nominal phrase: چرخ 'the sky/heaven', روان used adjectivally/participially as 'moving/flowing' or 'in motion', را is the definite/object marker attached to روان, and روش 'way/manner/course'. Together: 'thereupon the heaven's motion had its course' (i.e. the heavens followed their proper course).
segments.s064-g141: کس here functions as 'no one' (negative indefinite). از marks source/agent 'from' and داد یزدان is a genitival phrase 'the justice of God'. نیابد is a negative-present/subjunctive-form of یافتن 'to find' meaning 'does not/ cannot obtain' or 'does not escape'. گریغ is an archaic adjective/noun whose precise nuance is rare in modern Persian (sense unclear in the supplied list), used here as the thing that cannot be obtained/avoided with respect to God's justice.
segments.s064-g142: وگرچه is a concessive 'although/even though'. بپرد (from پرید) is an aoristic/optative-like verb meaning 'he may flee/leap/try to escape' (here hypothetical). برآید is a verb 'comes/arrives' or 'will come to' and combines with the preceding action to express result: 'even if he flees, he will come to...' به is the preposition 'to', and میغ is 'blade/edge' — i.e. 'to the blade' (he will still meet the sword).
segments.s064-g143: سنان‌گر is an agent-noun formed with -گر meaning 'spear-wielder' or 'one who uses a spear'. بدندان is an adverbial/phrase 'with teeth' or 'gnashing/clenching the teeth' (descriptive circumstance). بخاید is an archaic/poetic verb (here third-person) from خاییدن meaning 'to gape/ to open the mouth / be astonished' (used idiomatically for an open or forced mouth), used in the present/habitual/poetic sense. دلیر is a predicate/adjectival noun 'brave' describing the person.
segments.s064-g144: بدرد here is used in a poetic intransitive/passive sense 'is rent/is torn' or 'takes effect' (contextually 'is torn/rent'). ز is the classical preposition 'by/from/because of'. آواز او is the agentive/instrumental phrase 'his shout/cry' (آواز = voice/shout, او = his). چرم شیر is the object 'the lion's hide' (چرم = hide/skin, شیر = lion). Thus the clause says that by his shout the lion's hide is rent.
segments.s064-g177: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. نزدیکی گرگساران is a genitive phrase 'the vicinity/nearby of the wolves' (نزدیکی + possessor گرگساران). رسید is past tense 3rd-person singular 'he reached/arrived', so the whole segment reads 'when he reached the wolves' vicinity.'
segments.s064-g178: یکایک is an adverb 'one by one/one after another' modifying the following clause. ز دورش is a prepositional phrase 'from his distance / from afar' (ز = 'from', دور = 'distance/afar', + possessive ش = 'his' used idiomatically 'from afar'). سپهبد بدید is a past tense clause 'he saw the spahbed'; the subject is omitted (he).
segments.s064-g179: همی + گشت is a habitual or continuous past construction common in epic poetry ('he was roaming/kept turning'). گرد means 'around'. یکی کوهسار is an indefinite noun phrase 'a certain mountain' (یکی = 'a certain', کوهسار = 'mountain').
segments.s064-g180: چماننده is a comparative/adjectival participle 'resembling/like'. It modifies the preceding image: 'like a yūz'. یوز here is the hunting‑cat (cheetah) image; و links to the next phrase; رمنده شکار is a compound describing the prey as 'driven/fleeing quarry' (رمنده used as participial adjective of state, شکار = 'prey, quarry'). Together: 'like a cheetah and with the prey driven off.'
segments.s064-g181: چنین is an adverb 'thus/so'. گفت is the past tense verb 'he said' (here used as 'he spoke'); the provided lemma senses list did not include this verbal sense, so that sense is not available in the list. با introduces the comitative phrase 'with'. غمگساران خویش is 'his sorrowing companions' (غمگساران = 'companions in sorrow', خوی + ش = possessive formation meaning 'his').
segments.s064-g182: بدان کار is 'for that work/for that task' (ب = 'to/for', دان here functions in the demonstrative/compound to yield 'to that'). دیده is the past-participial/ adjectival form 'seen/chosen/ready' modifying سواران خویش 'his horsemen' — i.e. 'his horsemen appointed/ready for that task'. Word order places the participle before the noun it qualifies.
segments.s064-g183: که introduces a relative/quotative clause 'that/when/there came'. آمد is past tense 'came'. سواری دمان کابلی is a noun phrase 'a mounted rider at the dāman/foothill (دِمان used poetically for foot/skirt of the hill), a Kabulite' (کابلی = 'from Kabul'). Word order: head noun سواری followed by locative/epithet modifiers.
segments.s064-g184: چمان is an adverb/pronoun 'such/like this' that introduces description. چرمه is 'leather/leather garment' and زیر او = 'under him' (زیر = 'beneath', او = 'him') describes how it is worn. زابلی is a further epithet 'a Zābeli / from Zabul', completing the descriptive tag of the rider.
segments.s064-g153: ستاره شب تیره: a noun phrase where ستاره (star) is modified by شب تیره (the dark night). In Persian the adjective/possessive noun follows the head without an explicit -ِ (ezāfe) here because of poetic compression. یار منست is a predication: یار (companion/beloved) + منست, a poetic/contractional form corresponding to من است (‘is mine’ / ‘is my’), so the clause means “the star of the dark night is my companion.”
segments.s064-g154: من آنم که …: a common self-identifying formula “I am the one who …” — من (I) + آنم (literally ‘I am that’ used here as a demonstrative-copular element) followed by the relative clause introduced by که. دریا کنار منست is inverted relative clause: دریا (the sea) + کنار (beside/at the side) + منست (poetic = من است, ‘is mine / is at my side’).
segments.s064-g155: به رنجی رسیدستم از خویشتن — 'به' is the preposition 'to/into/with' introducing the state reached; 'رنجی' is an indefinite noun 'a suffering / a pain' (the -ی gives indefiniteness). 'رسیدستم' is an archaic 1st-person past form 'I reached / I came to' (رسیدن + personal ending -ستم). 'از خویشتن' ('from myself') gives the source or cause: 'I came to suffering from (because of) myself.' Word order shows the locative/goal phrase before the verb as typical in older poetic diction.
segments.s064-g156: که بر من بگرید همه انجمن — 'که' introduces the subordinate/result clause ('so that / such that / that'). 'بر من' is the prepositional phrase 'upon me / over me' and 'بگرید' is the classical past form 'they wept' (ب‑ prefix + گرِید/گریست root). 'همه انجمن' ('the whole assembly') is the subject; note the subject comes after the verb here because of the poetic word order and focus on the action (the assembly, as a whole, wept over me).
segments.s064-g157: اگرچه is a concessive conjunction meaning "although." دلم is دل + enclitic ـم ("my heart"); the enclitic marks possession. دید is the simple past "saw/perceived," and چندین ستم is the noun phrase "many/sundry injustices" serving as the object; the poetic word order places the verb between subject (دلم) and object (چندین ستم).
segments.s064-g158: نیارم here functions as an archaic/poetic first-person negative/modal form meaning roughly "I will not / I would not (wish to)" and takes the following infinitive زدن as its complement. جز introduces an exception "except," به فرمانت is a prepositional phrase "at/by your command" (ت = "your"), and دم (literally "breath") is used adverbially as "a moment/for even a moment."
segments.s064-g159: چه is the interrogative "what." فرماید is a third‑person present/future-form of فرمودن used here as "will say/says." اکنون "now" is an adverb placed before the subject. جهان‌پهلوان is the compound noun "world‑champion / mighty hero," placed after the verb by poetic inversion (subject following the verb).
segments.s064-g160: گشایم is a first‑person present/subjunctive/optative form from گشودن/گشا — here meaning "shall I open/relieve/freely set (something)." It governs the following prepositional phrase ازین (از + این) "from this," with رنج و سختی "suffering and hardship" as the source from which the speaker would free (روان) — روان is "soul/spirit" and is the affected noun at the end of the phrase. The sequence shows poetic ordering: verb + prepositional phrase + affected noun.
segments.s064-g185: فرستاده زال باشد درست is a hortative/optative statement choosing the envoy: فرستاده (messenger/envoy) + زال (proper name) is followed by باشد, the subjunctive/hortative form 'let it be / should be'. درست is an adverbial/postpositive qualifier ('properly/correctly' or 'indeed') that emphasizes the choice. The clause therefore declares who should be appointed as envoy.
segments.s064-g186: ازو آگهی جست باید نخست is ordered: ازو 'from him' marks the source, آگهی 'information' is the object, جست is the infinitival/past-stem of 'to seek' used here with the modal باید 'must' (باید follows the verb phrase) and نخست 'first' is the adverbial. Literally 'From him information to-seek must first' → 'First, one must seek information from him.'
segments.s064-g187: ز دستان و ایران و از شهریار strings together sources with the preposition ز/از 'from'. دستان (literally 'hands') is used here as a collective/metonymic source ('the leading hands' or chiefs), followed by ایران 'Iran' and شهریار 'the king'; the repeated prepositions emphasize each source. Word order is a simple list of origins introduced by ز/از.
segments.s064-g188: همی کرد باید سخن خواستار: همی (a habitual/intonation particle) + کرد (do/make) combines with باید (it is necessary/must) to express obligation ('one ought to do'). سخن (speech; here 'audience' or 'permission to speak') together with خواستار (one who requests) forms the object phrase 'a requester of speech' → 'seek an audience'. In short, the clause means 'one must also request to speak / ask for an audience.'
segments.s064-g189: هم اندر زمان پیش او شد سوار: هم 'also/at the same time' + اندر زمان 'in that time' sets the temporal frame; پیش او 'before him / in his presence' is the locative phrase, شد (past of شدن) indicates occurrence/arrival ('came/appeared'), and سوار 'a rider/horseman' is the person that arrived. The verb precedes the nominal predicate in this classical word order, yielding 'a horseman came before him.'
segments.s064-g190: به دست اندرون نامه نامدار is a circumstantial phrase describing what he carried: به دست 'in/with (his) hand' + اندرون 'inner/within' (here forming the fixed phrase 'دست اندرون' = 'in the hand') and نامه نامدار 'a famed/renowned letter' (نامه noun followed by the adjective نامدار). Together: 'with a famed letter in his hand.'
segments.s064-g191: فرود آمد و خاک را بوس داد: فرود آمد is a past action 'he descended / alighted', و links the actions, خاک را uses را as the definite object marker 'the earth/ground', and بوس داد is a periphrastic expression ('gave a kiss' = 'kissed') where بوس (kiss) is a noun used with داد 'gave'. The clause narrates two successive physical acts: he dismounted and kissed the ground.
segments.s064-g192: بسی از جهان‌آفرین کرد یاد: بسی 'greatly/much' (intensifier) + از 'of/from' introduces جهان‌آفرین 'the World‑Creator' (a name for God); کرد 'made/did' with the noun یاد 'remembrance' forms the periphrasis 'made remembrance' = 'remembered/commemorated'. The whole means 'he greatly remembered the Creator.'
segments.s064-g97: اگر introduces a conditional clause ('if'). شاه رابد is an archaic construction where رابد functions as a passive/affected verbal notion ('taken/seized') attached to شاه; in classical word order the verb-type element نگردد appears at the end of the clause. نگردد is the present subjunctive/optative form of گشت (here 'become/happen') negated by ن- yielding 'does not become / does not happen'. گمان begins the apodosis (the following main idea) and here means 'doubt/suspicion'.
segments.s064-g98: نباشد is the negative subjunctive/present negative used idiomatically as 'there is not / there should not be'. ازو marks source ('from him'). ننگ بر دودمان is a nominal phrase 'shame/disgrace upon the dynasty' with بر as the preposition 'upon' and دودمان 'lineage/dynasty'; together it states the feared consequence 'a disgrace upon the house'.
segments.s064-g99: یکی نامه is 'a certain letter / a letter' (literally 'one letter') placed before the predicate. باید is the modal 'must/ought' that governs the action implied (here the sending). سوی پهلوان is the prepositional complement 'toward/to the hero/champion' (سوی = 'to/toward'), so the whole segment means 'a letter should be sent to the champion.'
segments.s064-g100: چنان چون is a doubled comparative 'such as / just as'. تو دانی is a direct address 'you know' (دانی = present of دانستن 'to know'). به روشن‌روان is an epithet attached to the addressee — 'O bright‑souled one' — where به functions as a linking/prepositional particle introducing the epithet.
segments.s064-g101: ترا here is formed of the 2nd‑person clitic (ت) plus the object/topic marker را (thus topicalizing 'you'); in heroic/classical Persian this fronted form can function as a emphatic subject. خود خرد literally 'self‑wisdom' = 'your own wisdom' and is used predicatively; زان ما بیشتر is the comparative 'more than we' (زان = 'than/from that' introducing the comparison, ما = 'we', بیشتر = 'more').
segments.s064-g102: روان و گمانت links two items ('spirit/soul and your thought/opinion'); the suffix -ت on گمانت marks 'your'. به اندیشتر is a jussive/comparative admonition: به + comparative form اندیشتر yields 'be more thoughtful / be more deliberate' (i.e. exhorting greater care in spirit and judgment).
segments.s064-g103: مگر کو here introduces a rhetorical question akin to 'Is there not...?' (مگر used interrogatively). کو is an interrogative 'where / is there / who is', followed by یکی نامه 'some/a letter' and نزدیک شاه 'to/near the king' (نزدیک used spatially/transferentially = 'to the presence of / near'). The phrase probes for the existence of a person to perform the action.
segments.s064-g104: فرستد کند is a periphrastic verbal formation where فرستد (to send) is paired with کند (do/make) as an auxiliary to express potential/optative ('may send / could send'). رای او را نگاه is the object clause 'to see/learn his counsel' — رای 'counsel/opinion', او را with را as the object marker 'his', and نگاه here 'view/looking' used as 'to consult/learn' (i.e. 'to ascertain his opinion').
segments.s064-g129: ز مادر بزادم is a compact past statement: ز + noun (مادر) marks origin ('from/of my mother') and بزادم is an older past form meaning 'I was born' with the enclitic -م marking the 1st person. بران سان is a short adverbial phrase 'in/onto that manner' (بران + سان = 'in that way'), and که introduces the subordinate clause 'that…'; دید is the past tense main verb of that subordinate clause ('saw' / 'beheld'). Word order is typical older Persian: origin → verb (birth) → manner phrase → subordinating که → verb of the subordinate clause; the subject of دید is supplied by the following segment.
segments.s064-g130: ز گردون marks the source/agent ('from the گردون' — here poetic for fate/heaven), به من gives the experiencer/indirect object ('to me' / 'upon me'), بر ستمها is the prepositional phrase showing what fell upon the speaker ('upon injustices'), and رسید is the simple past ('reached' / 'came'). The verb is final, as expected; collectively this reads 'injustices came upon me from the heavens.'
segments.s064-g131: پدر is the subject ('father'). بود is the past of بودن ('was' / 'lived'), and در + a series of nouns ('ناز و خز و پرند') is a locative/idiomatic phrase meaning 'in (an atmosphere of) pampering/velvet/feathers' — i.e. 'in luxury, in silks and furs and plumes.' Coordinate nouns follow در, linked by و. This is a descriptive clause about the father's condition.
segments.s064-g132: مرا را برده سیمرغ بر کوه هند has a word order different from modern spoken Persian: مرا (the object 'me') appears before برده (the past participle/perfect 'taken'), سیمرغ (the agent) follows, and بر کوه هند indicates direction/place ('to Mount Hind'). را is the definite/object marker attached to the preceding clitic 'م' here (written split), برده is the participial/past form 'taken' (sense 'taken'), and بر + noun is the directional preposition phrase. Overall: 'the Simurgh had taken me to Mount Hind.'
segments.s064-g133: نیازم here presents an affected-state/experiential predicate involving the speaker (the clitic -م marks the experiencer). بد is an adverb/modifier meaning 'badly' / 'sorely' describing that state. آنکو introduces a relative/subordinate clause 'that he who…' and شکار آورد is a tight verb phrase 'brought (as) prey' (آورد = past 'brought'). Grammatically, the relative clause (آنکو شکار آورد) specifies the cause/content of the distress named by نیازم بد.
segments.s064-g134: ابا is the verb meaning 'to be unwilling / to refuse' (here in the past sense), بچه‌ام is the possessed noun 'my child' (بچه + enclitic -ام = 'my'), and در شمار آورد is an idiomatic verb phrase 'to put someone in the count / to reckon among' (آورد is past). Together the clause says he refused to reckon/include my child among (the group).
segments.s064-g135: همی is an adverb indicating habitual or continuous action ('continually' / 'always'). پوست 'skin' is the patient, از باد 'by/from the wind' gives the cause/agent, بر من marks the experiencer ('upon me'), and بسوخت is the past intransitive form 'was burned' (archaic/poetic prefixed form). Word order places the affected noun and agent before the verb, which comes final.
segments.s064-g136: زمان تا زمان is a reduplicated temporal expression 'time to time' = 'again and again / from time to time.' خاک (here 'dust') is the agent/subject of the following verb, چشمم is 'my eye' (چشم + enclitic -م), and بدوخت is the past 'sewed' / 'stitched' used figuratively (dust 'stitching' the eye shut or damaging it). The verb is final and the enclitic -م marks possession.
segments.s064-g169: سواری is the noun 'a rider/horseman' and serves as the subject. به کردار is an idiomatic prepositional phrase 'in the manner/guise of' (به + abstract noun کردار). آذر گشسپ is a personal name (here split into two words) that follows کردار to show whose guise it is: 'in the guise of Azar-Goshasp.' Word order is typical older Persian: modifier after کردار.
segments.s064-g170: ز کابل سوی سام شد is a motion clause: ز کابل 'from Kabul', سوی سام 'toward Sam', شد is the past of شدن marking the change/motion ('set off / moved'). بر دو اسپ ('on two steeds') is a postpositional phrase with بر meaning 'on/upon' and اسپ 'horse/steed'; the whole unit describes how he traveled.
segments.s064-g171: بفرمود is the honorific past verb 'he commanded/said'; و گفت reiterates 'and said' (a common rhetorical doubling). ار introduces a conditional ('if'), and بماند is 3rd-person present/subjunctive of ماندن 'to remain' used in the protasis. یکی is an indefinite 'one/someone' — 'if anyone should remain.'
segments.s064-g172: نباید is an impersonal modal 'must not/should not.' ت + را together mark the addressee/object 'for you' (the clitic t = 'you'; را the object marker). دم زدن is a verbal-nominal compound 'to breathe / to utter a breath/word' and here means 'to speak/utter.' اندکی 'a little' modifies دم زدن: 'not even a little (not even a single word).' The construction places the negated impersonal modal first, typical of older Persian.
segments.s064-g173: به دیگر is an adverbial phrase meaning 'again / once more.' تو is the vocative/subject 'you.' پای اندر آور is an imperative-like sequence 'bring/put (your) foot in' (پای + اندر + آور), with آور as a verb 'bring/put in.' برو is the imperative 'go.' The line contains commands addressed to 'you.'
segments.s064-g174: برین سان is an old demonstrative phrase (بر + این → برین) + سان 'so/like this' → 'in this manner.' همی is the continuous/progressive particle (here giving an ongoing sense) and تاز is the root of تازیدن 'to dash/gallop' — together همی تاز = 'keep galloping / press forward.' تا introduces the limit 'until,' and پیش‌گو is the addressee/goal 'the soothsayer.'
segments.s064-g175: فرستاده is the subject 'the envoy/sent one.' در پیش او is a locative phrase 'in front of him / before him' (در + پیش + pronoun). باد گشت is a metaphorical predication: باد 'wind' as predicate noun and گشت (past of شدن) 'became' — 'he became like the wind' i.e. he sped off.
segments.s064-g176: به زیر اندرش is a locative phrase 'beneath him / under him' (به + زیر + اندر + ش where ش is the 3rd-person possessive clitic 'his'). چرمه پولاد is a compound 'leather (armor) of steel' (چرمه 'leather/garment' + پولاد 'steel'), and گشت is 'became' — the clause says that what was beneath him turned into (or was like) steel, a poetic way to describe armor or transformation.
segments.s064-g89: همه موبدان is the subject ('all the mobeds/priestly class') followed by the object پاسخ ('answer/reply') and the past tense verb آراستند (3rd person plural). آراستند is a poetic/archaic past form of آراستن used here in the sense 'to arrange/compose' rather than literally 'to adorn.' Word order is Subject – Object – Verb, typical of Persian narrative style.
segments.s064-g90: همه (again 'all') begins the clause; کام و آرام او is a coordinated noun phrase ('his desire and his repose/peace') where او is the possessor. The verb خواستند is past 3rd plural 'they wished/desired'. The object phrase precedes the verb (Object – Verb). آرام here means 'calm/peace' rather than 'gentle' and کام means 'wish/desire.'
segments.s064-g91: که introduces a content clause ('that/for'), ما is the subject 'we.' یک به یک is an adverbial phrase 'one by one/each in turn.' بنده‌ایم is the predicative compound 'we are servants' (بنده + ایم = copula), and ترا (here split as مر + ت + را in the word list) functions as the dative/target 'to you/your' — the sequence is archaic/clitic: مر + ت + را together indicate relation to the 2nd person. Overall: 'that we are, one by one, servants to you.'
segments.s064-g92: The clause begins with the negator نه 'not.' از introduces the cause/ground ('because of'), بس modifies شگفتی to mean 'excessive/very great' astonishment, and سرافگنده‌ایم is a present/past-stative form 'we are ashamed' (here negated by نه). The whole means 'we are not ashamed on account of excessive wonder.'
segments.s064-g93: ابا آنکه functions as an idiomatic subordinating connector 'although/while' (derived from a verb of reluctance but used here as conjunction). مهراب is the proper name ('Mehrāb'). ازین پایه is a prepositional phrase 'from/of this base' (از + این (here spelled ین)), and نیست is the copula-negation 'is not.' Word order: subordinate conjunction – subject – prepositional complement – predicate.
segments.s064-g94: بزرگست is a nominal/adjectival predicate 'is great.' The coordinating و links further descriptors: گرد is another predicative adjective (an older/poetic adjective here acting to strengthen 'great'), then و introduces the negative clause سبک مایه نیست where سبک مایه is a compound noun 'light (in weight) substance/material' and نیست negates it. The clause thus contrasts greatness with the negated idea of being slight or of light material.
segments.s064-g95: بدانست is a past tense verb 'he knew/perceived.' کز (= که + ز) introduces an explanatory clause 'that/from which,' here functioning as 'that.' گوهر is 'jewel/gem' and اژدهاست is a nominal predicate 'is (of) the dragon' or 'belongs to the dragon' (the suffix -ست = 'is'). Together: 'he knew that the jewel was of the dragon.'
segments.s064-g96: وگر چند is an idiomatic concessive ('and yet / although') where وگر = 'and if/but if' and چند here supports the concessive sense; بر تازیان is a prepositional phrase 'over/upon the Arabs' (بر = over/against), and پادشاست is a nominal predicate 'he is king/ruler' (پادشاه with archaic copula -ست). So the clause reads 'and yet, though he was king over the Arabs.'
